events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes timing marks misaligned by a tooth on an automotive timing job. This is a mechanical timing alignment issue. Timing marks being off by a tooth is definitely out of tolerance - engine timing must be exact per OEM specifications. This could cause valve/piston interference, poor running, or engine damage.

Let me construct the JSON response.

- decision: fail (non-compliant, dangerous condition)
- risk: critical (misaligned timing can cause catastrophic engine damage)
- work_stage: rough-complete
- component_conditions: ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"]
- percent_complete: ~85 (roughed in but needs correction)
- value: 1 (one tooth off)
- confidence: high
- findings: timing marks misaligned by one tooth
- actions: re-time the engine, correct alignment
- rationale: timing must be exact per OEM specs, one tooth off is out of tolerance

{

  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Timing marks misaligned by one tooth — crank/cam synchronization is off specification",
    "One-tooth misalignment can alter valve timing enough to cause poor running, backfire, or interference-engine piston-to-valve contact"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Do not rotate engine or start vehicle until timing is corrected",
    "Remove timing belt/chain, realign all marks to OEM positions, re-tension, and verify alignment through two full crank rotations"
  ],
  "rationale": "OEM service information requires exact alignment of crank and cam timing marks. A one-tooth error is outside the zero-tolerance specification for engine timing and risks catastrophic mechanical damage, especially on interference engines.",
  "references": ["OEM service info — timing belt/chain alignment procedure and timing mark specification"]
}
